content.text:
### 💡 Summary of Key Points
- Every human contains a **Fitrah** (innate disposition to recognize God).
- The soul (**Ruh**) is of divine origin and blown into the body.
- The self (**Nafs**) can struggle between lower desires and higher morality.

--- 

### 📖 Deeper Explanation
**The Concept of Fitrah**
Islam teaches that children are born upon a pure, unconditioned nature. When humans reflect on creation, their soul automatically infers a supreme Creator.

**The Soul (Ruh)**
You are more than just biology. To complete the search for identity, one must feed the soul its spiritual sustenance through remembrance of Allah and prayer.

content.arabic: وَإِذْ أَخَذَ رَبُّكَ مِن بَنِي آدَمَ مِن ظُهُورِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ وَأَشْهَدَهُمْ عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ ۖ قَالُوا بَلَىٰ ۛ شَهِدْنَا
content.translation: And when your Lord took from the children of Adam from their backs their descendants and made them testify of themselves, 'Am I not your Lord?' They said, 'Yes, we have testified.'
Every child is born upon the fitrah.
